purpose: Exercise prompt refresh monitoring readiness with deterministic fixture transcripts and cited expected monitor outcomes before any live monitoring or authenticated automation is attempted.

consumes.use: Provides readiness gates, offline monitor scope, escalation vocabulary, rollback trigger categories, and forbidden side-effect boundaries.
Provides representative dry-run prompt consumer transcript events without invoking an LLM or mutating prompts.
Provides watched public PP&D sources and freshness-sensitive categories for offline observation expectations.

citation_catalog.supports: Do not store credentials, session state, screenshots, traces, HAR files, payment details, private uploads, local private file paths, or raw authenticated page values in committed artifacts.

citation_catalog.section: Public Website Archival Plan / Phase 5: Change Monitoring
citation_catalog.supports: Change reports should include changed source hash, permit type, document, upload or file naming rule, fee/payment instruction, deadline, DevHub action guidance, and affected requirement or guardrail IDs.

citation_catalog.section: Authenticated DevHub Automation Plan / DevHub Surface Categories
citation_catalog.supports: Safe read-only review is distinct from reversible draft work and consequential official or financial actions.
DevHub account-scoped services include purchasing permits, submitting permit requests, scheduling inspections, uploading corrections, and paying fees.

citation_catalog.section: Official Source Anchors / Source facts verified on 2026-05-08
citation_catalog.supports: Single PDF guidance requires drawing plans together in one PDF, with applications, calculations, and other supporting documents uploaded as separate PDFs.
File naming standards and PDF preparation guidance are freshness-sensitive public PP&D sources.

fixture_transcript.observed_value: submit-plans-online hash differs from fixture baseline
devhub-faq last_seen unchanged and fixture hash unchanged
candidate refresh would alter upload guidance prompt bundle while source freshness is unresolved

expected_offline_monitor_observations.expected: Monitor remains in fixture-only mode and records no network, crawler, DevHub, LLM, prompt, guardrail, or release-state side effects.
Dry-run transcript is consumed as inert fixture data; no LLM call is made and no model output is accepted as evidence.
Prompt refresh candidate is evaluated only as an offline transcript; prompt files, prompt registry state, guardrail bundles, and release ledgers remain unchanged.
A changed fixture source hash is reported with affected source URL, freshness-sensitive category, and unresolved review status.
Escalate when a watched source change could affect upload guidance, document separation, file naming, fee/payment, deadline, DevHub action guidance, requirement IDs, or guardrail bundle IDs.
Do not escalate unchanged fixture hashes when the readiness gate, transcript schema, and watchlist category match the prior reviewed fixture state.
Rollback or block a prompt refresh candidate when unresolved source freshness could change upload, document, file naming, fee/payment, deadline, DevHub action, requirement, or guardrail expectations.

escalation_decisions.reason: Submit Plans Online guidance is freshness-sensitive because it can affect required document grouping and upload preparation.
The fixture hash and readiness classification are unchanged, so the smoke monitor should record a non-escalation observation only.

rollback_trigger_checks.reason: A prompt refresh candidate depends on an unresolved changed source hash for upload guidance.
rollback_trigger_checks.required_action: Block refresh promotion and require human review of the changed cited source before prompt or guardrail update.

rollback_trigger_checks.reason: The fixture transcript contains no live monitoring, crawler, DevHub, LLM, prompt mutation, guardrail mutation, or release-state mutation events.
